content:
The user wants me to extract the content of the provided image into a JSON format. **1. Analyze the image:** The image is a page from a technical datasheet, specifically for the STUSB4500 device. - **Header:** ST logo, STUSB4500, Pin description. - **Section Title:** 2.2.8 POWER_OK2 / POWER_OK3 - **Text:** Describes the pins, their default status, configurations, and behavior. - **Table:** "Table 4. POWER_OK pin configuration". It contains columns for Configuration ID, Pin name, Value, and Description. - **Footnotes/Additional Text:** Below the table, there are notes and bullet points explaining specific behaviors. **2. Extract Content:** * **Header:** * Logo: ST * Part Number: STUSB4500 * Description: Pin description * **Section 2.2.8:** * Title: POWER_OK2 / POWER_OK3 * Text: "These pins report by default the status of the USB power delivery contract negotiation with the source. Different configurations are proposed as stated in the table below to meet specific application requirements. The configuration of the POWER_OK pins can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration). Depending on the programmed configuration, they can be used in combination with VBUS_EN_SNK pin to enable different power path scenarios. POWER_OK2 pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable a power path. POWER_OK3 is a low voltage open drain output." * **Table 4:** * Title: Table 4. POWER_OK pin configuration * Columns: Configuration ID /NVM parameter PWR_OK_CFG[1:0], Pin name, Value, Description * Rows: * Header row. * "Configuration 1: all PDOs on single VBUS power path" * 00b -> VBUS_EN_SNK(1) -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No source attached / Source attached * 00b -> POWER_OK2 -> Hi-Z -> No functionality * 00b -> POWER_OK3 -> Hi-Z -> No functionality * "Configuration 2: all PDOs on single VBUS power path + dedicated high power charging paths" * 10b (default) -> VBUS_EN_SNK(1) -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No source attached / Source attached * 10b (default) -> POWER_OK2 -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No PD explicit contract / PD explicit contract with PDO2 * 10b (default) -> POWER_OK3 -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No PD explicit contract / PD explicit contract with PDO3 * "Configuration 3: all PDOs on single VBUS power path + detection of USB Type-C current capability from source" * 11b -> VBUS_EN_SNK(1) -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No source attached / Source attached * 11b -> POWER_OK2 -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No source attached or source supplies default USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached / Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached * 11b -> POWER_OK3 -> Hi-Z / 0 -> No source attached or source supplies default USB Type-C current at 5V when source attached / Source supplies 1.5 A USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached * 01b -> (empty) -> (empty) -> Not applicable * **Notes below table:** * 1. The VBUS_EN_SNK pin values correspond to the default behavior * In case of configuration 2 (default): * Bullet points explaining PDO negotiation and source request behavior. **3. Format as JSON:** Construct the JSON structure based on the extracted content. ```json [{"bbox": [68, 66, 126, 90], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [813, 66, 930, 96], "category": "header", "text": "STUSB4500\nPin description"}, {"bbox": [67, 120, 398, 134], "category": "title", "text": "2.2.8 POWER_OK2 / POWER_OK3"}, {"bbox": [161, 137, 886, 193], "category": "text", "text": "These pins report by default the status of the USB power delivery contract negotiation with the source. Different configurations are proposed as stated in the table below to meet specific application requirements. The configuration of the POWER_OK pins can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."}, {"bbox": [161, 195, 929, 221], "category": "text", "text": "Depending on the programmed configuration, they can be used in combination with VBUS_EN_SNK pin to enable different power path scenarios."}, {"bbox": [161, 223, 929, 250], "category": "text", "text": "POWER_OK2 pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable a power path."}, {"bbox": [162, 252, 498, 267], "category": "text", "text": "POWER_OK3 is a low voltage open drain output."}, {"bbox": [406, 286, 687, 300], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Table 4. POWER_OK pin configuration"}, {"bbox": [164, 305, 927, 794], "category": "table", "text": "
Configuration ID /NVM parameter PWR_OK_CFG[1:0]Pin nameValueDescription
Configuration 1: all PDOs on single VBUS power path
00bVBUS_EN_SNK^(1)Hi-ZNo source attached
0Source attached
POWER_OK2Hi-ZNo functionality
POWER_OK3Hi-ZNo functionality
Configuration 2: all PDOs on single VBUS power path + dedicated high power charging paths
10b (default)VBUS_EN_SNK^(1)Hi-ZNo source attached
0Source attached
POWER_OK2Hi-ZNo PD explicit contract
0PD explicit contract with PDO2
POWER_OK3Hi-ZNo PD explicit contract
0PD explicit contract with PDO3
Configuration 3: all PDOs on single VBUS power path + detection of USB Type-C current capability from source
11bVBUS_EN_SNK^(1)Hi-ZNo source attached
0Source attached
POWER_OK2Hi-ZNo source attached or source supplies default USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached
0Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached
POWER_OK3Hi-ZNo source attached or source supplies default USB Type-C current at 5V when source attached
0Source supplies 1.5 A USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached
01bNot applicable
"}, {"bbox": [164, 798, 591, 811], "category": "table_footnote", "text": "1. The VBUS_EN_SNK pin values correspond to the default behavior"}, {"bbox": [162, 823, 401, 837], "category": "text", "text": "In case of configuration 2 (default):"}, {"bbox": [162, 840, 920, 867], "category": "text", "text": "- When a PDO negotiation succeeds, the POWER_OK pin related to the negotiated PDO is enabled (active low) when PS_READY message is received from the source"}, {"bbox": [162, 869, 926, 896], "category": "text", "text": "- When a new PDO is negotiated upon source request, the active POWER_OK pin is disabled (Hi-Z) when the STUSB4500 sends an RDO (request data object) message to the source with the new negotiated PDO"}, {"bbox": [68, 973, 164, 984], "category": "footer", "text": "DS12499 - Rev 8"}, {"bbox": [872, 974, 930, 986], "category": "footer", "text": "page 7/42"}] ```
